games.title: Cuddle Buddies
games.rules: Take turns drawing a card from any of the three decks. Use the prompt as inspiration for a cuddly moment. Consent is important. Only proceed with an action if everyone agrees to do so.

decks.label: The Friendzone
prompts: Sit back-to-back and relax together for 1 minute
Link arms while walking across the room
Rest your head briefly on their shoulder
Give each other a high-five that turns into holding hands for 5 seconds
Sit side-by-side with your legs touching
Lean your knees against each other while sitting
Give a 10-second one-armed side hug
Stand shoulder-to-shoulder and sway gently for 20 seconds
Gently tap or trace shapes on their hand — they guess what it is
Sit cross-legged facing each other, knees touching
Share a blanket over both your laps
Rest your hand lightly on their forearm during a conversation
Mirror each other’s posture and maintain eye contact for 30 seconds
Give a slow, deliberate handshake that lingers
Sit close enough that your hips touch and stay there for a minute
Place your hand on top of theirs and breathe together for 5 breaths
Lightly bump shoulders and stay leaned in
Let your feet touch under the table for 1 minute
Gently guide them by the elbow across the room
Do a “trust lean” where you gently lean into each other while standing

decks.label: Cuddle Buds
prompts: Sit with one person leaning fully into the other’s chest
Side hug that turns into a full 20-second hug
Sit between their legs while both are upright
Rest your head in their lap for 1 minute
Wrap up together in a blanket cocoon
Sit facing each other with legs intertwined
One person gently strokes the other’s arm for 30 seconds
Lay side-by-side with your heads touching
Give each other a slow, full hug without talking
Sit on the couch with one person’s legs draped over the other
Lean your head against theirs and stay still
Sit behind them and gently hold their shoulders
“Half spoon” while sitting (one person slightly behind, arms around)
Hold each other in a quiet embrace for 1 minute
One person plays with the other’s hair
Lay with your legs tangled together
Sit chest-to-chest and match breathing
One person rests their hand on the other’s back
Sit on the floor with one person leaning fully into the other’s torso
Try a full spooning position for 1–2 minutes

decks.label: Borderline Foreplay
prompts: Sit straddling their lap (clothed) and maintain eye contact
Slow, close full-body hug with hips aligned
Sit between their legs and gently lean back into them
Rock gently together while sitting close
Lay partially on top of each other (torso-to-torso)
Wrap legs around each other while sitting
Sit facing each other, legs intertwined, bodies close
Slow grinding motion while both fully clothed (keep it subtle and playful)
Lay with one leg draped fully over their hips
Sit on their lap and lean chest-to-chest
Full-body spooning with tighter contact
Press foreheads together while bodies stay fully connected
Gentle hip-to-hip movement while hugging
Lay between their legs while facing them
Sit straddling one thigh and lean forward
Hold each other tightly while slowly shifting weight together
Sit close enough that your entire bodies are touching and move together slightly
One person pulls the other closer by the waist and holds
Slow, playful “almost grinding” with exaggerated pauses
Stay in a close straddle position and synchronize breathing and movement

games.title: WNRS
games.rules: Each player draws one card per round, choosing freely from any of the three decks. Reflect quietly for a moment before answering — depth is rewarded over speed. Others may ask one follow-up question after each answer. When a Wildcard is drawn, the question is posed to the entire group simultaneously. Each person writes their answer before anyone speaks — then all answers are revealed at once.

prompts.text: What do you value above all else in life?
Describe a time you acted against your own values — what happened?
What belief have you held longest, and has it ever wavered?
Everyone writes down one thing they would never compromise on — then reveals.
Name something you would never compromise on.
How do you want to be remembered?
What fear has held you back the most?
Describe a challenge that changed you fundamentally.
What's a mistake you've learned the most from?
When do you find it hardest to be honest?
Everyone shares one thing they find hardest to accept about themselves.
What would your younger self be surprised to know about you now?
Who has influenced you the most, and how?
Describe a moment of unexpected kindness you experienced.
What makes you feel truly seen by another person?
Everyone completes this sentence: 'I feel most connected when…'
What's something you wish you'd said to someone?
What does home mean to you?

Each person writes their answer before anyone speaks — then all answers are revealed at once.
